title: Sample-conditioned candidate-set prompt evidence routing

queries: G-Retriever query relevant subgraph retrieval graph prompt
GraphRAG query focused graph retrieval evidence selection

papers.title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Graph Question Answering
papers.claim: Graph prompt evidence should be retrieved according to the query rather than consumed as a generic compressed graph context.
papers.relevance: EXP180 used only a global candidate-set query. A sample-conditioned candidate query is closer to query-focused graph retrieval while preserving the same prompt length and scorer.

papers.title: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
papers.claim: GraphRAG separates graph-guided retrieval from generation and emphasizes query-relevant evidence selection.
papers.relevance: Stage 3 needs a different information path from final scorer variants. Query-focused evidence routing is still Stage4-compatible through proxy candidate-ranking/value-selection episodes.

evidence.why_now: EXP180 failed fixed screening but reached item-incoterms mrr=0.7432317302922772 with acceptable throughput, above the repeated scorer-collapse bands. Its routing query was candidate-set-only and therefore could not distinguish which candidate-relevant evidence mattered for the current sample. Query-focused GraphRAG/G-Retriever evidence plus EXP180's retune-plausible signal justify one non-near-duplicate routing follow-up before retiring this evidence-routing branch.

hypothesis: For autocomplete multiclass tasks, candidate-set-only prompt routing is too generic. Combining the seed/sample prompt token representation with the candidate-set query should route existing graph evidence toward evidence that is both candidate-relevant and sample-relevant. This keeps Stage 3 finetune-only, does not inflate prompt length, does not change the scorer, avoids manual top-k/token-budget scans, and preserves a Stage 4 proxy candidate-ranking training story.
notes: Draft created after EXP180 finalized failed but retune-plausible.
Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not use --pretrain.
This is not a scorer near-variant: it changes only graph prompt evidence ordering before frozen-LLM consumption.
Do not scan routing top-k, prompt token budget, residual scales, rank margins, auxiliary weights, batching, or chunk sizes.

Implementation plan: reuse EXP180 candidate-set query, add seed/sample prompt representation from the existing sample_head, route existing graph prompt tokens by the normalized sum of sample and candidate queries, and keep token count unchanged.
Pre-launch validation completed on 2026-05-11: py_compile, git diff --check, JSON validation, and remote item-incoterms shape/throughput sanity all passed.

Remote sanity check completed on 2026-05-11: a 64-step item-incoterms finetune-only run on lab25211 GPU6 completed cleanly, reached roughly 6.2 train step/s with batch_size=4 (about 24.8 effective train items/sec), and eval/test ran around 12.1 it/s with val_size=1.
Final metrics: EXP183 user-churn roc_auc=0.6877176117513276; EXP184 user-ltv mae=79.40659794325009; EXP185 item-incoterms mrr=0.6782075427827381.
Final verdict under replay-aware effective deltas: user-churn better, user-ltv neutral, item-incoterms worse; global verdict failed.
Throughput was acceptable: EXP185 ran on two ranks with per-rank batch 2 and visible training around 6.5 step/s, about 26 effective train items/sec; final test ran around 12.17 it/s with val_size=1.

Interpretation: adding sample/seed conditioning to the candidate-set routing query hurt salt-side ranking and returned item-incoterms to the known weak collapse band, despite improving user-churn. This is not a promotable candidate and is weaker than EXP180's candidate-set-only routing signal.

Search-space consequence: do not pursue sample-conditioned prompt routing as the next manual branch. If continuing the evidence-routing line, preserve candidate-set-only guidance and reduce order destruction by routing metadata groups/blocks rather than individual prompt tokens; no top-k, token-budget, residual-scale, or scorer-neighbor scans.
